text: I feel a curious detachment here. The sleek, glass-walled architecture and glowing screens evoke an atmosphere of observation—a space designed for both scrutiny and innovation. When I step closer to the research wall with its bright R+D lettering and layered screens, it makes me ponder my own role in this dance of visibility and participation. Am I the observer or the observed? Surrounded by symbols of knowledge and inquiry, I sense both the excitement of discovery and a subtle self-consciousness, as if every gesture might be a data point. This ambiguity makes me reflect on the boundaries between public and private, between what is created and what is seen.
